query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "full attention strikes back: transferring full attention into sparse w" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (d) as it investigates the same core mechanism—transferring attention patterns from a pre-trained model to a student model—albeit in the domain of Vision Transformers rather than the specific linguistic context mentioned in the user's title. It serves as a foundational empirical study on the "sufficiency of attention maps" for knowledge transfer, which is the exact mechanism the user's proposed work ("Full Attention Strikes Back") aims to extend to linguist
